title: Hieracium tricheilema
intro: Hieracium tricheilema, also known as Tricholepis, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. Traditionally, Hieracium tricheilema has been used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium tricheilema include sesquiterpene lactones, such as tricheilemone, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Hieracium tricheilema is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat digestive issues and as a topical application to soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Hieracium tricheilema is a species within the flowering plant genus Hieracium, which is commonly known as hawkweed. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, which includes many other common flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers. This genus is also known as Pilosella in some classifications, which can cause confusion. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium tricheilema is part of the subfamily Cichorioideae. Its classification can also be expressed in a more detailed format using the binomial nomenclature system, where it is referred to as Hieracium tricheilema.
